title: ipv6_addr_to_str uses snprintf but doesn't document uncompressed format choice
description:
ipv6_addr.c:184-194 formats IPv6 addresses as uncompressed hex (fe80:0000:0000:...). The header (ipv6_addr.h:67-71) documents this, but the implementation doesn't explain WHY this format was chosen over RFC 5952 compressed form.

What goes wrong: Someone might 'fix' it to use the shorter compressed format, breaking log parsing or display assumptions elsewhere in the system.

Suggested addition to implementation:
```c
/* 
 * Uncompressed format chosen for:
 * 1. Deterministic output (same address = same string)
 * 2. Simpler parsing (no :: expansion needed)
 * 3. Consistent log line lengths
 */
```
